hyperedges.label: Shopping List Ecosystem (Lists, AI Categories, Output Modes, Pantry)
Weekly Plan Workflow (Plan, Swap, Preferences, Snapshot, Links)
Deployment Environments (Dev, EC2, Mac Mini, Prod Override, GHCR Build)

nodes.label: Lightweight bot check: verifies the Telegram webhook route exists in this app.
nodes.norm_label: lightweight bot check: verifies the telegram webhook route exists in this app.

nodes.label: Only checks whether an AI key env var is present.     No external call unless pr
nodes.norm_label: only checks whether an ai key env var is present.     no external call unless pr

nodes.label: Scheduler health: checks if we have a recent heartbeat stored in DB.     We keep
nodes.norm_label: scheduler health: checks if we have a recent heartbeat stored in db.     we keep

nodes.label: Extended metrics for the health dashboard.
nodes.norm_label: extended metrics for the health dashboard.

nodes.label: Returns (picked_recipe_ids, dummy_titles_if_missing)     - Picks from DB first (
nodes.norm_label: returns (picked_recipe_ids, dummy_titles_if_missing)     - picks from db first (

nodes.label: GHCR :stable Pointer Tag
nodes.rationale: Immutable sha-<sha> tags plus a movable :stable pointer let EC2 auto-pull the latest build without compose changes; rollback is just retagging :stable to an older sha.

nodes.label: DB Change Guard Workflow
nodes.rationale: Prod DB (Supabase) has no auto-migrations, so CI blocks PRs that touch backend/app/models.py or main.py without adding a new db/migrations/*.sql file.

nodes.label: Backend Python Dependencies (FastAPI, SQLModel, psycopg, openai)
nodes.norm_label: backend python dependencies (fastapi, sqlmodel, psycopg, openai)

nodes.label: Supabase SQL Migration Policy
nodes.rationale: Migrations are append-only manual SQL applied in Supabase SQL Editor because prod must not rely on runtime ORM auto-create.

nodes.label: EC2 systemd Timer Auto-Pull Deployment
nodes.rationale: A systemd timer on EC2 pulls every 2 minutes and compares image digests, so deployment needs no push access to the server — merging to main is the only trigger.

nodes.label: Mac Mini Production Server
nodes.rationale: Runs the whole stack (frontend, backend, Postgres, Caddy with real TLS) locally on the Mac Mini to replace EC2 + Supabase; Supabase is paused, not deleted, as fallback. Supabase Session Pooler is used for migration because the direct connection is IPv6-only and often fails from Docker.

nodes.label: DuckDNS Dynamic DNS via launchd
nodes.rationale: A launchd job reports the home IP to DuckDNS every 5 minutes so the domain survives dynamic IP changes; Caddy then obtains a Let's Encrypt certificate automatically.

nodes.label: Supabase Data Migration (make migrate-supabase)
nodes.rationale: Dumps the full public schema from Supabase into local Postgres and verifies row counts per table; repeatable (--clean) so it can be re-run at cutover to pick up interim changes.

nodes.label: Per-Use-Case OpenAI Model Config (OPENAI_MODEL / OPENAI_MODEL_ACTIVITIES)
nodes.rationale: Backend selects the model per use case instead of one global model: gpt-4o-mini for recipes/shop, a stronger model for the activities ideas generator.

nodes.label: Feature Brief: Was unternehmen wir heute? (Activities Ideas Generator)
nodes.norm_label: feature brief: was unternehmen wir heute? (activities ideas generator)

nodes.label: Activities Settings in app_state JSON
nodes.rationale: Settings are persisted as JSON inside app_state to avoid DB schema changes and migrations for per-feature configuration.

nodes.label: Feature Brief: AI Smart Shopping List (aggregate + sort by impact)
nodes.norm_label: feature brief: ai smart shopping list (aggregate + sort by impact)

nodes.label: Shared Shop AI Transformation Service (shop_ai)
nodes.rationale: Implemented once in backend business logic so GUI endpoint and Telegram handler produce identical shop output; must never invent items and must fall back to the non-AI list when OPENAI_API_KEY is missing or validation fails, so shopping never breaks.

nodes.label: Feature Brief: Build Date on Homepage (BUILD_DATE build-arg)
nodes.norm_label: feature brief: build date on homepage (build_date build-arg)

nodes.label: Feature: Finanzen / Fixkosten Dashboard MVP
nodes.rationale: No household_id because Family Ops has no real multi-household model; monthly incomes are month-scoped rows rather than static settings so shared and per-person views can coexist; existing Split logic left untouched.

nodes.label: Feature Brief: Git SHA in Backend + Frontend (Homepage)
nodes.rationale: Build metadata comes from CI build-args (GITHUB_SHA) with a safe local fallback so builds stay deterministic and the running commit is always visible for prod verification.
nodes.norm_label: feature brief: git sha in backend + frontend (homepage)

nodes.label: Feature Brief: Landing Page + Use Case Entry
nodes.norm_label: feature brief: landing page + use case entry

nodes.label: Use-Case Landing Page Pattern (clickable use-case cards on /)
nodes.rationale: The landing page is a hub of equally styled use-case boxes (first: Kuechen- & Wochenplan, moved to /kueche) so future use cases can be added in the same style; invisible placeholder boxes reserve their space.
nodes.norm_label: use-case landing page pattern (clickable use-case cards on /)

nodes.label: Recipe URL AI Import
nodes.rationale: Preview must not write to DB; only explicit user confirm saves. created_by forced to 'dennis' server-side. No DB schema extension allowed; copyright-safe: never store full instructions, only short German summary.

nodes.label: SSRF Protection for URL Fetch
nodes.rationale: Block localhost/private/link-local IPs, non-http schemes, URL userinfo; limit redirects, bytes, timeouts; require text/html.

nodes.label: Prompt Injection Defense
nodes.rationale: Webpage content is untrusted data; system instruction tells model to only extract recipe fields and ignore embedded instructions.

nodes.label: Duplicate Prevention by Canonical URL
nodes.rationale: Check source_url == canonical final URL on preview and again on save to avoid race conditions; MVP blocks duplicates instead of allowing override.

nodes.label: Import Preview Cache (app_state)
nodes.rationale: Cache preview by URL hash with 10-30 min TTL to avoid repeated OpenAI calls (cost/rate limiting).

nodes.label: OpenAI Structured Outputs (JSON Schema)
nodes.rationale: Structured Outputs guarantee valid JSON drafts; JSON-LD schema.org Recipe parsed first, readability fallback second.

nodes.label: Recipe Archiving (Soft Delete)
nodes.rationale: Keep DB row, set is_active=false to hide from UI; no restore UI in MVP, reactivation is manual in DB; default list keeps returning only active recipes.

nodes.label: Birthday Feb-29 Leap Year Fix
nodes.rationale: Birthday helpers extracted into a testable module so Feb-29 birthdays do not crash in non-leap years; Telegram birthday output uses the same robust date logic.

nodes.label: Expense Stable Member IDs
nodes.rationale: Additive fields paid_by_member_id and split_among_member_ids replace name-based attribution; legacy rows stay name-based, new rows are stable. Additive SQL migration instead of breaking change.

nodes.label: Open Balance KPI Correction
nodes.rationale: open_balance now sums all open transfers instead of only the largest single amount.

nodes.label: Next.js Standalone Config Consolidation
nodes.rationale: next.config.ts sets output standalone; contradictory next.config.js removed so the standalone Docker build is reliable.

nodes.label: SystemStatus Component (Health Block)
nodes.rationale: Health block moved from home page to end of settings page, extracted as reusable frontend component.

nodes.label: Weekly Plan Recipe Links (source_url priority)
nodes.rationale: External source_url wins; fallback to internal /recipes/[id]; weekly API extended additively with days[].source_url, no schema change.

nodes.label: Pantry / Basisvorrat
nodes.rationale: Pantry items excluded from shopping list but usage kept transparent via three groups: BUY, PANTRY_USED, PANTRY_UNCERTAIN_USED; uncertain flag marks items that might be missing.

nodes.label: Weekly Plan Preferences Bias (Tags)
nodes.rationale: Max 50% of weekly plan days match selected tags (3 of 7); no preferences means unchanged mixed behavior; must apply to both GUI endpoint and Telegram plan command via same functions.

nodes.label: Telegram Auto-Send Settings (GUI-triggered)
nodes.rationale: notify=1 query param defaults to 0 so other callers stay unchanged; sends only if setting enabled AND last_chat_id exists; last_chat_id stored on every bot update.

nodes.label: app_state JSON Settings Storage
nodes.rationale: All settings persisted as JSON strings in public.app_state (keys settings_pantry, settings_preferences, settings_telegram, telegram_last_chat_id) to avoid DB schema changes.

nodes.label: Ingredient Normalization
nodes.rationale: Lowercase/trim/collapse-whitespace plus pantry alias map; synonym expansion only via pantry aliases, conservative German plural heuristic optional.

nodes.label: Shop Output Mode Toggle (ai_consolidated / per_recipe)
nodes.rationale: Default keeps AI consolidated behavior with non-AI fallback; per_recipe shows ingredients grouped per recipe without consolidation, stable day order.

nodes.label: Shared Shop Output Formatter (Backend Service)
nodes.rationale: Formatting implemented once in backend; GUI shop endpoint and Telegram shop handler must call the same formatter so both channels stay consistent.

nodes.label: AI Categories for Shopping Lists
nodes.rationale: Categories derived dynamically from existing ingredients; hard server-side validation that AI never rewrites, removes, or invents ingredients; manual entries stay before categorized recipe items.

nodes.label: shopping_list_items.category Column
nodes.rationale: Additive DB column persisting AI category per item; migration must be applied in Supabase before prod deployment.

nodes.label: Einkaufsliste Use Case (Shopping Lists)
nodes.rationale: Multiple persistent lists replace the old weekly-plan SHOP UI; manual entries always ordered before imported recipe ingredients; /api/weekly/shop endpoint kept for now although the weekly-plan UI shop was removed.

nodes.label: Weekly Plan Snapshot Import
nodes.rationale: Lists are snapshots, not live references synced with the weekly plan; reuses existing weekly-plan shop logic for import.

nodes.label: AI Cost Estimation (CHF/EUR)
nodes.rationale: Uses OPENAI_MODEL_ACTIVITIES (same model as activity ideas); rough estimate only, price level per Switzerland (CHF) or Germany (EUR); currency switch deliberately discards old estimates until re-estimated.

nodes.label: Swap Wizard (Weekly Plan Day Swap)
nodes.rationale: Fixes preview not opening after Generate Preview; iterative re-roll of selected days; persist weekly plan only on Confirm; random selection across all active recipes instead of newest-biased.

nodes.label: Session-Scoped Swap Avoid List
nodes.rationale: Rejected suggestions must not reappear within one swap session; cleared on confirm/discard; stored preferably in weekly_plan_drafts jsonb or app_state.

nodes.label: Shared Swap Logic (GUI + Telegram)
nodes.rationale: Swap generation implemented once in backend business logic and used by GUI endpoints and Telegram handlers; no per-channel logic paths.

nodes.label: Telegram Create-Event Notifications
nodes.rationale: Per-content-type toggles (recipe, plan, chore, shopping list, expense, pinboard, birthday, family member); double-message guard: with notify=1 and auto_send_plan active the existing full-text plan message wins over the compact event.

nodes.label: Telegram Mobile UI Expansion (Inline Menus)
nodes.rationale: Main menu with inline buttons and step-by-step creation flows; recipes are read-only in the bot; reuses GUI logic and existing APIs; legacy commands not hard-broken.

nodes.label: Weekly Plan GUI (Plan / Swap / Shop)
nodes.rationale: Reuses existing Telegram bot behavior exactly; no new business logic, no schema changes; current week only (Monday-based).

nodes.label: /api/weekly/* REST Wrapper Endpoints
nodes.rationale: Thin adapters (current/plan/swap/confirm/cancel/shop) calling the same internal helpers as the Telegram handler (_build_new_week_plan, _apply_swaps, _format_plan, draft helpers); REST endpoints must not send Telegram messages.

nodes.label: weekly_plan_drafts (Swap Draft State)
nodes.rationale: Swap stores a draft proposal requiring confirm (apply to weekly_plans, delete draft) or cancel (delete draft).

nodes.label: Chunked AI Categorization for Large Lists
nodes.rationale: Prod fix: large lists split into chunks with longer timeouts; frontend now treats ok:false as an error and keeps the loaded list instead of hanging on 'Lade...'.
nodes.norm_label: chunked ai categorization for large lists

nodes.label: Build Images (GHCR) Workflow
nodes.rationale: On push to main, builds and pushes frontend and backend images to ghcr.io tagged :latest and :sha with GHA build cache.

nodes.label: SSH Test (EC2) Workflow
nodes.rationale: Manual workflow_dispatch smoke test that SSH access to the EC2 host and its Docker install work.

nodes.label: EC2 Compose Stack
nodes.rationale: Caddy TLS on 80/443 in front of GHCR :stable images; no local DB (DATABASE_URL points to external Supabase); AUTO_MIGRATE=0 in prod.

nodes.label: Mac Mini Compose Stack
nodes.rationale: Fully local 'prod': builds images from source, includes local Postgres 17 (chosen so pg_dump/restore works against any current Supabase version); AUTO_MIGRATE=0.

nodes.label: Prod Compose Override
nodes.rationale: Override swapping local builds for GHCR :stable images and disabling the local db service via the 'dev' profile; AUTO_MIGRATE=0.

nodes.label: Local Dev Compose Stack
nodes.rationale: Caddy on host port 8080 with Caddyfile.local; source builds; local Postgres 16 with init scripts; AUTO_MIGRATE=1 for local dev only.

nodes.label: Home Page Dark Theme Background Gradient Asset
nodes.rationale: Smooth gradient background transitioning from warm brown/orange tones at top through purple/magenta midpoint to cool deep blue-purple, fading to near-black at bottom. Provides aesthetic depth and visual hierarchy for dark mode home page. V2 iteration indicates design refinement.
nodes.norm_label: home page dark theme background gradient asset

nodes.label: Dark Theme Design System
nodes.rationale: App supports both light and dark color schemes. Dark theme uses warm-to-cool gradient backgrounds to provide visual polish while maintaining readability and modern aesthetic.

nodes.label: Gradient Background Design Pattern
nodes.rationale: Multi-stop gradient aesthetic employed for home page backgrounds. Design choice: smooth color transitions create depth, guide visual attention, and establish premium visual tone without relying on imagery.

nodes.label: Home Page Light Theme Background
nodes.description: Soft gradient background for light theme home page. Warm color palette featuring orange, yellow, and peach tones blending into cream/white. Creates a welcoming, warm visual foundation with organic, flowing gradient shapes and low opacity blurred circular elements.

nodes.label: Light Theme Color Palette
nodes.rationale: Establishes warm, inviting visual identity for light theme UI. Uses organic gradient transitions and soft color blocking to create approachable, non-technical aesthetic suitable for family-focused household management application.

nodes.label: Gradient Background Pattern
nodes.rationale: Design system pattern: soft, blurred gradient backgrounds with overlapping circular/organic forms provide visual depth and visual interest while maintaining readability. Reduces visual weight through low opacity and blur effects.

nodes.label: Orange-Gold Glow with Dark Background and Teal Accents
nodes.norm_label: orange-gold glow with dark background and teal accents

nodes.label: Dark Mode App Icon Design Pattern
nodes.norm_label: dark mode app icon design pattern

nodes.label: Premium Badge Icon
nodes.rationale: Visual representation of a premium tier badge showing achievement level (50 points). Design uses warm orange-to-red gradient for premium quality association, 3D glossy rounded square for visual depth and modern UI appeal, and metallic chrome '50' text for prestige.

nodes.label: Logo Premium Dark
nodes.rationale: Premium dark-mode variant of Family Ops logo; uses neon aesthetic with orbital connectivity pattern to convey tech-forward, interconnected home management system

nodes.label: House Icon Symbol
nodes.rationale: Central focal point; represents home/family core of the app; dual-color gradient (cyan to gold) symbolizes balance between tech intelligence and warm family values

nodes.label: Orbital Network Pattern
nodes.rationale: Two concentric rings with connected nodes; represents interconnected systems, continuous operation, and data/automation flowing around central home concept; suggests networked family operations

nodes.label: Neon Glow Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: Modern, premium positioning; neon glow conveys technology, sophistication, and forward-thinking approach to household management

nodes.label: Dual-Color Gradient System
nodes.rationale: Cyan/teal (outer ring, house left) = trust, intelligence, technology; Gold/warm (inner ring, house right) = value, warmth, family; gradient blend represents unified cool-tech + warm-family duality

nodes.label: Dark Theme Optimization
nodes.rationale: Deep space background with stars; optimized for dark mode UIs; reinforces premium, modern aesthetic; high contrast with glowing elements

nodes.label: Flowing Wave Visual Pattern
nodes.rationale: Organic flowing ribbons suggest motion, premium smoothness, and continuous flow - conveying fluidity and luxury tier experience

nodes.label: Cosmic Starfield Background
nodes.rationale: Dark navy with scattered white particle points creates sense of expansiveness and premium tier significance - unlimited possibilities

nodes.label: Teal-Amber Color Palette
nodes.rationale: Complementary teal/cyan and warm amber/gold tones create visual contrast and depth, suggesting premium feature tier visual distinction

nodes.label: Dark Theme Design Choice
nodes.rationale: Deep dark background (high contrast) creates premium, sophisticated aesthetic and reduces eye strain for hero banner prominence

nodes.label: Premium Tier UI Asset
nodes.rationale: Hero banner specifically designed to visually communicate premium/elevated tier status in application UI

nodes.label: Family Ops Repo Rules for AI Agents
nodes.norm_label: family ops repo rules for ai agents

nodes.label: Local DEV vs PROD (EC2 GHCR / Supabase)
nodes.norm_label: local dev vs prod (ec2 ghcr / supabase)

nodes.label: Prebuilt graphify knowledge graph
nodes.rationale: Query the graph before scanning code — connections cost ~9x fewer tokens than a file scan.

nodes.label: Database policy (SQL migrations single source of truth)
nodes.rationale: Migrations are append-only and safe to apply to PROD; do not rely on runtime ORM auto-create in production.
nodes.norm_label: database policy (sql migrations single source of truth)

nodes.label: Hard restrictions (no Kubernetes, no local Supabase)
nodes.norm_label: hard restrictions (no kubernetes, no local supabase)

nodes.label: Mandatory agent orchestration
nodes.rationale: Decompose non-trivial work into one orchestrator plus focused sub-agents to reduce risk and improve parallelism.

nodes.label: Knowledge Graph (query first, saves tokens)
nodes.rationale: Answers and connections in graphify-out cost ~9x fewer tokens than a broad file scan.

nodes.label: Stack (Next.js 16, React 19, FastAPI, SQLModel, Postgres, Caddy)
nodes.norm_label: stack (next.js 16, react 19, fastapi, sqlmodel, postgres, caddy)

nodes.label: Database schema changes / migrations
nodes.rationale: Schema changes require a migration file — CI enforces this.

nodes.label: AUTO_MIGRATE=1 (local dev only)
nodes.rationale: Never enable AUTO_MIGRATE on prod.

nodes.label: Git & branch workflow
nodes.rationale: Merging to main triggers GitHub Actions building GHCR images and EC2 auto-pulls :stable.

nodes.label: Family Ops design consistency
nodes.rationale: Do not treat backend, frontend, and Telegram as separate products; integrate coherently.

nodes.label: DEV/TEST and PROD environments on Mac Mini
nodes.norm_label: dev/test and prod environments on mac mini

nodes.label: Two Git worktrees (DEV and PROD)
nodes.rationale: PROD stack builds exclusively from the PROD worktree fixed on main, so uncommitted/untested DEV code can never accidentally go live.

nodes.label: Full isolation (containers, networks, DB volumes, ports)
nodes.rationale: Docker isolates by project name; collisions only on host ports, and DEV (8080) and PROD (80/443) do not overlap.
nodes.norm_label: full isolation (containers, networks, db volumes, ports)

nodes.label: make dev-seed (fill DEV DB from PROD backup)
nodes.rationale: Copies data only from the PROD backup into the DEV DB; the live DB is never touched.
nodes.norm_label: make dev-seed (fill dev db from prod backup)

hyperedges.label: Shopping List Ecosystem (Lists, AI Categories, Output Modes, Pantry)
Weekly Plan Workflow (Plan, Swap, Preferences, Snapshot, Links)
Deployment Environments (Dev, EC2, Mac Mini, Prod Override, GHCR Build)
